schematics.title: Litematica schematics
schematics.hint: Load the schematic with the Litematica mod in Minecraft to rebuild the project block by block.

litematica.title: Set up & use Litematica
litematica.intro: Quick-start and FAQ around the Litematica mod so you can use the schematics from this overview right away.

entries.a: Litematica is a Minecraft client mod (Fabric) that displays schematic files (.litematic) as a hologram in-game. You can rebuild any project 1:1 without touching the server — other players don't see your hologram.

entries.q: How do I install the mod?
entries.a: You need the Fabric loader for your Minecraft version and drop the Litematica JAR (plus its dependency MaLiLib) into the mods/ folder. After launch, check the mods menu to confirm both are active.

entries.q: How do I load a schematic?
entries.a: Save the .litematic file in .minecraft/schematics/. In-game, open the Litematica menu (default: M), pick 'Load schematic' and set the position via 'Schematic placement'.

entries.q: What's the point / setup guide for?
entries.a: Each schematic ships with a recommended origin and facing direction. Following them places the hologram in the intended spot and aligns it with neighbouring builds.

entries.q: Can I use the schematics on my own world?
entries.a: Yes, of course. The schematics in this overview are free to use privately and on your own servers. Please credit the original builders when sharing publicly.
